{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2404584?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geo-chen/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geo-chen/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geo-chen/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geo-chen/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/geo-chen", "id": 2404584, "login": "geo-chen", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjI0MDQ1ODQ=", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geo-chen/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geo-chen/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geo-chen/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geo-chen/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geo-chen/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/geo-chen", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-53755", "cvss": {"score": 8.6, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.6, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-918", "name": "Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)"}], "description": "### Summary

The Docker API server applied its SSRF destination check to the crawl target URL only, not to the proxy address. An unauthenticated request could supply a proxy pointing at an internal IP and route the browser through it, reaching internal services and cloud-metadata endpoints, while using a perfectly valid crawl URL. The Docker API is unauthenticated by default.

### Affected paths

`/crawl`, `/crawl/stream`, and `/crawl/job` accept a `browser_config` (and `crawler_config`). The following all feed Chromium's egress and were unchecked:
- `browser_config.proxy_config.server`
- `browser_config.proxy` (deprecated field)
- `crawler_config.proxy_config.server`
- `--proxy-server` / `--proxy-pac-url` / `--proxy-bypass-list` / `--host-resolver-rules` flags in `browser_config.extra_args`

### Attack

An attacker sends `/crawl` with a benign, validation-passing URL but a `proxy_config.server` pointing at an internal IP. Chromium routes all requests through that proxy. For plain-HTTP targets the proxy receives the full request and can return any content, which is then returned verbatim in the crawl result (`results[0].html` / `cleaned_html` / `markdown`). In a real deployment the proxy would be an attacker-controlled server pointing at cloud metadata (e.g. AWS IMDSv1 at 169.254.169.254) to retrieve IAM credential tokens.

### Impact

Unauthenticated server-side request forgery to internal services and cloud-metadata endpoints, with the response returned to the attacker.

### Fix

Every proxy destination is validated with the same global-routability check used for crawl URLs (reject any resolved address that is not `is_global`, including IPv6 transition forms) before the browser is constructed; proxy/DNS-redirecting flags are stripped from `extra_args`. A legitimate public proxy still works. Honors `CRAWL4AI_ALLOW_INTERNAL_URLS`.

### Workarounds

- Upgrade to the patched version (0.8.9).
- Enable authentication (`CRAWL4AI_API_TOKEN`).
- Restrict the container's outbound network access (egress firewall / no metadata route).

### Credits

Geo ([geo-chen](https://github.com/geo-chen)) - reported the proxy_config.server SSRF with a clear PoC.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00289, "percentile": 0.2055}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-6qhc-x826-342c", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-16T21:02:55Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6qhc-x826-342c", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-6qhc-x826-342c"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-53755"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-16T21:02:55Z", "references": ["https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/security/advisories/GHSA-6qhc-x826-342c", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6qhc-x826-342c"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/unclecode/crawl4ai/security-advisories/GHSA-6qhc-x826-342c", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai", "summary": "Crawl4AI: SSRF via proxy settings in the Docker server bypasses the crawl-URL SSRF check", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-16T21:02:55Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-6qhc-x826-342c", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "0.8.9", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "crawl4ai"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 0.8.8"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Crawl4AI: SSRF via proxy settings in the Docker server bypasses the crawl-URL SSRF check
